behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real LLM output content (signature correctness), which a stub would mask. MOCK-HIDE and LLM-DEP are both yes. Rejected Workflow: behavior only requires one model decision (initial generation), not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real generation model with a spec that strictly defines parameter names/types/return type, parse the produced signature, and assert exact interface match; repeat across runs/prompts to catch drift/non-deterministic signature mistakes.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is module handoff/control-flow correctness (orchestrator + test-result evaluator + attempt loop). Real OS/network/model quality is not required. Rejected System: no behavior text requiring real subprocess/network. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: assertion is ordering/early-stop logic, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic stub generator and stub test runner returning fail on attempt 1, pass on attempt 2, and verify call order, attempt count, and no attempt 3 invocation.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple real agentic steps in sequence (generate -> test fail -> refine -> retest -> pass). Correctness depends on model decisions across attempts, so Agentic floor is exceeded by MULTI-STEP.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full live loop on a known prompt where first-attempt pass rate is low; assert that at least one run reaches pass within <=3 attempts and that pass occurred after at least one failed prior attempt.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundary (test result serialization -> prompt builder -> next request payload). No real model output quality required. Rejected Agentic: behavior checks prompt payload contents, not whether model uses it correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic failing test results and inspect the next prompt payload sent by orchestration; assert each failed test name and message is present and correctly mapped.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Needs sequential real model decisions and cross-attempt context carryover (initial code + failure context + refined code). A stub can trivially change text and hide true adaptation failures. MULTI-STEP is yes.
behaviors.test_description: Run live attempt 1 to failure, feed real failure context into refinement, run attempt 2 with real model, and assert attempt-2 code is materially different and aligned to failed assertion area (not just whitespace/renaming).

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is orchestration/bookkeeping across modules. Real model quality and real OS/network are unnecessary for this failure mode. Rejected Workflow/Agentic: behavior is stop condition and error structure, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: With deterministic stubs forcing three failures, verify loop terminates exactly at 3, response is structured error, and payload contains all 3 attempt artifacts and corresponding test results.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Even with stubbed model outputs, this behavior explicitly includes end-to-end test execution wiring; real subprocess/filesystem execution paths are where integration fakes often miss failures (exit codes, temp files, runner invocation). Rejected Agentic/Workflow: explicitly non-agentic and deterministic stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end pipeline with stubbed generator but real local test execution path (actual runner/subprocess and temp workspace) and assert expected attempt bookkeeping and final status.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a multi-step live agent loop with sequential model decisions and feedback incorporation; both LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP are yes.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real loop on a curated hard task with known first-attempt failure tendency; assert attempt 1 fails, later attempt passes within limit, and intermediate artifacts show real refine/retest progression.
